findings.evidence: `rg -n "MultInt|intEquivOfZPowersEqTop|intEquivZMultiples"` finds `MultInt` only in this PR, while Mathlib has `Mathlib/GroupTheory/SpecificGroups/Cyclic.lean:530: noncomputable def intEquivOfZPowersEqTop ... : Multiplicative ℤ ≃* G` and this PR has `TauCeti/Algebra/Group/ZMultiples.lean:28: noncomputable def intEquivZMultiples ... : ℤ ≃+ zmultiples p`.
findings.fix: Rename these to an `Int`-based name such as `addCircleMulEquivInt` / `addCircleMulEquivInt_apply`, matching Mathlib's `intEquivOfZPowersEqTop` and TauCeti's `intEquivZMultiples`.
findings.issue: `addCircleMulEquivMultInt` and `addCircleMulEquivMultInt_apply` use the nonstandard abbreviation `MultInt`, which is ambiguous and has no precedent for `Multiplicative ℤ`.

summary: One introduced equivalence uses nonstandard `MultInt` terminology for `Multiplicative ℤ`; the surrounding Mathlib/TauCeti API names this with `Int`/`toMultiplicative`, not `MultInt`.
